howItWorks.intro: This calculator estimates ADU construction costs based on the type of build, square footage, location, and finish level. It also projects potential rental income and return on investment.
rules: Garage conversion: $100–$200/sqft (existing structure, needs insulation, plumbing, finishes)
Detached new build: $150–$350/sqft (full construction from foundation up)
Basement conversion: $80–$180/sqft (existing structure, may need egress, waterproofing)
howItWorks.outro: ADU costs vary significantly by region. California and New York can be 50-100% more expensive than national averages. Permit costs and timelines also vary — some cities have streamlined ADU permitting while others require lengthy review processes. Always get multiple contractor quotes.

faq.question: How much does it cost to build an ADU?
faq.answer: ADU costs typically range from $80,000 to $250,000+. Garage conversions run $60,000-$120,000, basement conversions $50,000-$100,000, and detached new builds $100,000-$300,000+. Costs vary enormously by location — a detached ADU in Los Angeles averages $200,000-$350,000, while in a mid-cost area it might be $120,000-$200,000.

faq.question: What is the cheapest type of ADU to build?
faq.answer: Basement conversions are typically cheapest ($80-$180/sqft) since the structure already exists. Garage conversions are next ($100-$200/sqft). Both leverage existing foundations, walls, and roofs. Detached new builds are most expensive but offer the most design flexibility and often command higher rents.

faq.question: How long does it take to build an ADU?
faq.answer: Typical timelines: garage conversions take 3-5 months, basement conversions 2-4 months, and detached new builds 6-12 months. Add 2-6 months for permitting and design before construction begins. Prefab/modular ADUs can reduce build time to 2-4 months after site prep.

faq.question: Do ADUs increase property value?
faq.answer: Yes. ADUs typically increase property value by 20-35% of their construction cost, and often more in high-demand rental markets. A $150,000 ADU might add $100,000-$200,000 in property value, plus generate $1,500-$2,500/month in rental income. The income potential often makes the ROI very attractive.

faq.question: What permits do I need for an ADU?
faq.answer: Requirements vary by jurisdiction but typically include: building permit, planning/zoning approval, utility connections (sewer, water, electric), and sometimes a separate address. California has streamlined ADU permitting statewide. Permit fees range from $2,000-$15,000 depending on location and ADU type.

faq.question: Can I build an ADU on my property?
faq.answer: In most US cities, yes — especially after recent zoning reforms. California, Oregon, and Washington have statewide ADU legalization. Check your local zoning for: minimum lot size, setback requirements, maximum ADU size (often 800-1,200 sqft), height limits, and owner-occupancy requirements. Many cities have relaxed these rules significantly since 2020.
